federal_land_actions.description: Convey selected National Forest System lands out of federal ownership subject to reservations, easements, and closing conditions.
Acquire non-federal lands for National Forest System management, access, habitat, recreation, and consolidation benefits.
Reserve public access, trail, road, mineral, and resource-protection interests where needed.

geography: Bridger, Bangtail, and Crazy Mountains Geographic Area
Madison, Henrys Lake, and Gallatin Mountains Geographic Area
project_name: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Land Exchange Proposed Action
proposed_action_summary: The Forest Service proposes a land exchange on the Custer Gallatin National Forest involving federal land disposal, non-federal land acquisition, access easements, deed restrictions, mineral reservations, trail and road access commitments, and resource-protection conditions. The action would affect National Forest System lands and lands entering federal ownership in the Crazy Mountains area.

proposed_action_elements.description: Exchange federal and non-federal parcels in the East Crazy Mountains and Inspiration Divide areas on an equal-value basis.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA proposed action basis for federal and non-federal parcel exchange and equal-value land exchange processing.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Transfer, split, reserve, or update water rights associated with federal and non-federal parcels.

evidence_refs.citation_label: ECID Final EA Section 2.2.2 and Table 3
evidence_refs.summary: Final EA proposed action basis for water-right transfer, split ownership, reservation, amendment, or no-change findings.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Move parcels into or out of the Crazy Mountain Inventoried Roadless Area, Crazy Mountains Backcountry Area, Madison Roadless Area, and South Crazy Mountain Recommended Wilderness context.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for analyzing roadless, backcountry, and recommended-wilderness ownership context.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Reserve road and trail easements, construct Sweet Trunk Trail No. 274, relocate Inspiration Divide Trail No. 8, and improve Big Timber Canyon Trailhead.

evidence_refs.citation_label: ECID Final EA Section 2.5 trails and access
evidence_refs.locator: Section 2.5 New Trail and Trailhead Improvement; Patent Reservations
evidence_refs.summary: Final EA proposed action basis for trail construction, trail relocation, trailhead improvement, easements, and patent reservations.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Review affected lands special use authorizations, recreation special use authorizations, and outfitter-guide permits.

evidence_refs.citation_label: ECID Final EA Section 2.5 special uses
evidence_refs.locator: Section 2.5 Land Special Use Authorizations and Recreation Special Use Authorizations
evidence_refs.summary: Final EA proposed action basis for lands special uses, recreation special uses, and outfitter-guide permit effects.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Retain or restrict high-value wetlands, riparian areas, and monitoring access through boundary changes and deed restrictions.

evidence_refs.locator: Section 2.3 Alternative 1 Wetlands Protection and Balance
evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for retaining wetlands, applying deed restrictions, and preserving monitoring access.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Analyze ownership changes to perennial streams, trout-bearing stream miles, lake acres, and recreational fishing opportunities.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for stream, lake, trout-bearing aquatic-resource, and recreational fishing analysis.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Analyze habitat and consultation implications for grizzly bear, Canada lynx, wolverine, whitebark pine, big game, and other wildlife.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for wildlife, big game, grizzly bear, Canada lynx, wolverine, whitebark pine, and habitat analysis.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Analyze at-risk plants and whitebark pine effects associated with trail work, land ownership change, and management commitments.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for at-risk plant, whitebark pine, botanical survey, and mitigation analysis.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Protect identified cultural resources on federal lands leaving federal ownership through patent reservations and monitoring access.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for cultural-resource protection, patent reservations, and monitoring access.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Consult with Tribal Nations regarding the Crazy Mountains tribal cultural landscape, sacred sites, treaty rights, and traditional cultural practices.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for Tribal consultation, Crazy Mountains tribal cultural landscape, sacred sites, and traditional cultural practices.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Resolve federal and non-federal mineral estate ownership, outstanding mineral rights, mineral potential, and BLM concurrence.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for mineral estate ownership, outstanding rights, mineral potential, and BLM concurrence.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Identify affected grazing allotments, term permits, fences, roads, irrigation ditches, and related improvements.

evidence_refs.locator: Section 2.5 Livestock Grazing Permits and Leases
evidence_refs.summary: Final EA proposed action basis for affected grazing allotments, permits, leases, and improvements.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Complete hazardous-materials and site-condition due diligence before conveyance.

evidence_refs.citation_label: ECID Final EA Section 2.5 hazardous materials
evidence_refs.summary: Final EA proposed action basis for hazardous-materials and site-condition due diligence before conveyance.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Address climate-change context and carbon storage or sequestration implications for the land exchange.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for climate-change context and carbon storage or sequestration analysis.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_elements.description: Track scoping, public comments, agency coordination, interested parties, permits, and administrative review requirements.

evidence_refs.summary: Final EA basis for agency consultation, public involvement, administrative review, and interdisciplinary team documentation.
evidence_refs.title: East Crazy Inspiration Divide Final Environmental Assessment January 2025.pdf

proposed_action_basis: Equal-value land exchange and land adjustment decision pathway
resource_analysis_expectations.resource_area_name: Lands, realty, and land exchange case

proposed_action_basis: General Exchange Act, FLPMA, land exchange regulations, and Forest Service land exchange policy
Project consistency with Custer Gallatin Land Management Plan direction
Water rights transfer, split ownership, reservation, amendment, or no-change findings
Ownership changes in roadless, backcountry, and recommended wilderness contexts
Road, trail, easement, trailhead, and access commitments
Dispersed recreation, hunting, fishing, trail, snowmobile, and trailhead effects

Wildlife habitat, big game, lynx, grizzly bear, wolverine, and other species effects
ESA consultation and listed/proposed species determinations
Perennial streams, trout-bearing streams, lake acres, and recreational fishing effects
Wetland retention, deed restrictions, floodplains, and riparian resources
Construction, road/trail, stream, wetland, and water-quality implications
Historic properties, archaeological sites, patent reservations, and monitoring access
Tribal cultural landscape, treaty rights, sacred sites, and government-to-government consultation
Federal and non-federal mineral estate ownership, outstanding rights, mineral potential, and BLM concurrence
At-risk plants, whitebark pine, and botanical mitigation or survey commitments
Climate-change context and carbon storage or sequestration analysis
Hazardous-materials and site-condition due diligence before conveyance
Affected grazing allotments, permits, fences, roads, irrigation ditches, and other improvements
Land special use authorizations affected by lands leaving or entering federal ownership
Scoping, agencies/persons consulted, public involvement, and administrative review
Agency and person consultation roster needed for the project record
